mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS rate relative to its trailing 6-month average signals reduced discount-rate pressure on equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following flat/declining FEDFUNDS periods are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE rose >0.3pp in 3 months, 60-day forward SPX returns were positive, or the effect is not significant at p > 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in RSAFS growth and HOUST decline signals a joint weakening of consumer and housing demand that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as aggregate demand expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND HOUST 3-month change < -5%
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST weakness signal fails to produce negative mean 20d returns distinct from either single-series signal at p > 0.10, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not incremental over univariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect expanding industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as near-term earnings revisions turn favorable.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated (above 4%) and CPIAUCSL inflation is moderating on a year-over-year basis, markets price in an approaching easing cycle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as forward-looking discount rates decline in anticipation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth declining for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal reverses sign in more than one historical tightening-peak episode.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE constitutes a stronger recession-risk signal than either series individually, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as labor market slack accumulates faster than headline data suggests.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change < 100k AND UNRATE up > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE trigger produces negative mean 20d returns in fewer than 55% of historical instances, or interaction model Rank IC is not superior to univariate UNRATE model at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in HOUST exceeding 8% signals an abrupt contraction in housing starts that acts as a leading indicator of consumer confidence deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term risk sentiment shifts.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following HOUST declines > 8% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, invalidating the short-horizon reaction hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above its 12-month moving average), a simultaneous rise in UNRATE amplifies recession risk pricing because the central bank has less room to ease preemptively, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. In easing regimes the same UNRATE rise is offset by expected policy support and the effect weakens or reverses.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month average AND UNRATE rising > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: The negative 60-day return effect fails to be significantly stronger in tightening regimes versus easing regimes (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), or reverses sign in more than one historical tightening episode.
